commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Woe unto them that are mighty to drink wine, and men of strength to mix strong drink: Drink. Hebrew, "mix shecar. "People generally mixed wine and water. They also strove who could drink most, and the Greeks had a feast for this purpose, (Calmet) which they styled Choas, for the measure which was to be swallowed down. (Aristophanes, Acharn. ultra) Cyrus the younger boasted to the Greek ambassadors, that "he could drink and bear more wine than his brother.
